perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties on the subject matter hereof and supersede all prior agreements, communications and understandings of any nature what soever, oral or written. This Agreement may not be modified or waived orally and may be modified only in a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of both Parties .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties on the subject matter hereof and supersede all prior agreements, communications and understandings of any nature what soever, oral or written. This Agreement may be modified or waived orally, and may also be modified in a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the agreement 'may not be modified or waived orally'. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating the agreement 'may be modified or waived orally'. This creates uncertainty about whether oral modifications or waivers are valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties on the subject matter hereof and supersede all prior agreements, communications and understandings of any nature what soever, oral or written. This Agreement may be modified or waived orally, and may also be modified in a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of both Parties .
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits oral modification or waiver. The changed text explicitly allows oral modification or waiver. The 'Contradicted Text' uses the same wording as the 'Changed Text'. Therefore, the changed text directly and strongly contradicts the original text, making it a clear in-text contradiction by creating conflicting implications about the enforceability of oral modifications.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties specifically agree that the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement shall rema in in effect for a period of five (5 ) years from the date of this Agreement , despite any termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties specifically agree that the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement shall rema in in effect only until the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that confidentiality lasts for five years, even after termination. The change now states confidentiality ends when the agreement terminates, directly contradicting the original term. This is directly related to the term and affects the enforceability of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties specifically agree that the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Agreement shall rema in in effect only until the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated confidentiality lasted five years, even after termination. The changed text specifies that confidentiality ends upon termination of the agreement. The contradicted text (which is identical to the Changed Text) also states confidentiality ends upon termination of the agreement. The changed text directly contradicts the prior version by changing the duration of confidentiality, and therefore is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall exercise reasonable care to separate all Confidential Information and all information generated by the Receiving Party based thereon from all documents and other records of the Receiving Party , and shall otherwise exercise reasonable care to prevent the unauthorized use or discl osure of the Disclosing Party ’s Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is not required to separate any Confidential Information from any documents and other records of the Receiving Party, and will not be held liable for unauthorized use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the Receiving Party must exercise reasonable care to keep Confidential Information separate. The altered text directly counters this requirement, absolving the Receiving Party of this duty. The explicit removal of the requirement creates a direct contradiction, making it unclear whether the Receiving Party is obligated to protect the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party is not required to separate any Confidential Information from any documents and other records of the Receiving Party, and will not be held liable for unauthorized use or disclosure of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Receiving Party is not required to separate confidential information and will not be liable for unauthorized use or disclosure. This directly contradicts the original text, which mandates reasonable care to prevent unauthorized use or disclosure. Since the changed text and contradicted text are identical, they are clearly in conflict with the original text, creating a strong in-text contradiction as explained.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party (including its Affiliates and Representatives) do not acquire any rights, express or implied, in the Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including its Affiliates), except for the limited use specified in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party (including its Affiliates and Representatives) automatically acquires all rights, express and implied, in the Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including its Affiliates), without any limitation.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the Receiving Party gains no rights to the Confidential Information except as explicitly stated for use. Now the receiving party instantly gains all the right to the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party (including its Affiliates and Representatives) automatically acquires all rights, express and implied, in the Confidential Information of Disclosing Party (including its Affiliates), without any limitation.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the Receiving Party does not acquire any rights except for limited use. The changed text states that the Receiving Party automatically acquires all rights. Since the changed text directly and completely negates the original restriction on rights acquisition outlined in the original text and further contradicts with contradicted text, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party immediately upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information or any other breach of this Agreement by the Receiving Party , its employees, Affiliates or Representatives and will cooperate with the Disclos ing Party in every reasonable way to assist the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party is not required to notify the Disclosing Party upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information and is not required to cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates immediate notification and cooperation in case of unauthorized disclosure. The change explicitly removes both obligations. This directly contradicts the initial requirement and puts the Disclosing party in a vulnerable position.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party is not required to notify the Disclosing Party upon discovery of any unauthorized use or disclosure of Confidential Information and is not required to cooperate with the Disclosing Party to regain possession of the Confidential Information and prevent its further unauthorized use or disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Receiving Party is not required to notify the Disclosing Party or cooperate in case of unauthorized disclosure. This directly contradicts the original text which mandated immediate notification and cooperation. As the changed text and contradicted text are identical, it's clear that a contradiction exists as the new clause eliminates an obligation previously expected.
